_notes: v1.1.0 (2026-05): no event changes — bump tracks the CLI side adding
`matcher_mode` (restrictive|all|custom) + comprehensive debug logging
for diagnosing permission_mode + matcher coverage issues.
PreToolUse: fires BEFORE Claude Code invokes a tool. Returning
permissionDecision=allow/deny/ask is the only way to gate a tool.
Claude Code only blocks on the hook when permission_mode is the
default 'plan'/'default' mode; if the user has flipped to
acceptEdits / bypassPermissions / skip, the tool runs regardless,
so winmux's `pre-tool-use` short-circuits with allow in those modes
(otherwise the user would see a dead card they can't act on).
Notification: fires for Claude Code's UI toasts (waiting on input,
permission-pending, etc.). Passive — we just log it; no decision.
SessionStart/SessionEnd: lifecycle hooks for session create / teardown.
Passive — useful for analytics, banner injection, etc.
Stop: fires when the assistant finishes a turn. winmux uses it to
trigger the optional Claude auto-summary feature (Phase 17).
Matcher of `*` matches every invocation of that event. The PreToolUse
matcher is intentionally narrower — restricting which tools surface
a winmux card. Override with `--matcher-mode all` (or the Settings
→ Hooks → 'Match all tools' toggle) to route EVERY tool through
winmux. Use `custom` if you've hand-edited ~/.claude/settings.json
and don't want setup-hooks to overwrite the matchers.
